events.text: Patient is a 71-year-old female of Black/African American race, non-Hispanic ethnicity, with a BMI of 23.8 and a past medical history of coronary artery disease with prior stent placement and hyperlipidemia.

At age 71, patient presented with new-onset epigastric pain radiating to the back, early satiety, and a 12-pound weight loss over six weeks; CT pancreas protocol demonstrated a 4.8 cm mass in the body of the pancreas (C25.1) encasing the celiac artery and abutting the superior mesenteric artery greater than 180 degrees, classified as locally advanced unresectable pancreatic adenocarcinoma.

At age 71, CT abdomen/pelvis with pancreas protocol revealed a 4.8 cm hypodense mass centered in the pancreatic body with encasement of the celiac artery and greater than 180-degree contact with the SMA; upstream pancreatic duct dilation to 7 mm; no hepatic metastases; several borderline enlarged peripancreatic lymph nodes measuring up to 1.4 cm in short axis.

At age 71, EUS performed showing a 4.6 x 3.9 cm hypoechoic mass in the pancreatic body with celiac artery encasement confirmed; FNA performed with multiple passes using 22-gauge needle; rapid on-site evaluation confirmed adequate cellularity.

At age 71, EUS-guided FNA of pancreatic body mass: cytology demonstrates markedly atypical glandular cells arranged in disorganized sheets and single cells with high nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratio, coarse chromatin, and frequent mitoses; consistent with poorly differentiated adenocarcinoma (histology code 8140/3); immunohistochemistry positive for CK7 and CA19-9, negative for CK20 and CDX2.

At age 71, next-generation sequencing performed on FNA specimen: KRAS G12V mutation detected (variant allele frequency 41%), TP53 Y220C mutation, SMAD4 loss, BRCA2 pathogenic variant c.6174delT (germline confirmed on subsequent testing), PALB2 wild-type, microsatellite stable (MSS), tumor mutational burden 6 mutations/Mb.

At age 71, CA 19-9 at diagnosis was 1,247 U/mL; CEA 9.8 ng/mL; clinical staging cT4N1M0, Stage III; ECOG performance status 1; multidisciplinary tumor board recommended neoadjuvant FOLFIRINOX with consideration for platinum sensitivity given BRCA2 mutation; genetic counseling referral placed.

At age 71, pain management initiated with celiac plexus neurolysis performed under EUS guidance for severe epigastric and back pain; patient reported significant pain improvement from 8/10 to 3/10 on visual analog scale.
At age 71, nutritional assessment showed albumin 3.1 g/dL and prealbumin 14 mg/dL; started on oral nutritional supplements and pancreatic enzyme replacement therapy; referred to registered dietitian.

At age 71, patient began FOLFIRINOX (5-fluorouracil 2400 mg/m2 46-hour infusion, leucovorin 400 mg/m2, irinotecan 150 mg/m2, oxaliplatin 85 mg/m2) every 14 days; dose-attenuated with irinotecan reduced to 135 mg/m2 given age and cardiac history; cycle 1 of planned 12 cycles.
At age 71, after cycle 3 of FOLFIRINOX, patient developed grade 2 nausea and grade 2 fatigue; required IV hydration and addition of olanzapine to antiemetic regimen.

At age 71, after cycle 6 of FOLFIRINOX, patient developed grade 2 peripheral neuropathy with numbness and tingling in fingers and toes affecting fine motor tasks; oxaliplatin discontinued from regimen.
At age 71, mid-treatment CA 19-9 decreased from 1,247 to 389 U/mL after 6 cycles; treatment continued without oxaliplatin (modified FOLFIRI).

At age 71, restaging CT after 8 cycles showed decrease in pancreatic body mass from 4.8 cm to 3.1 cm; persistent celiac artery encasement though with improved fat plane around SMA; no new metastatic disease; stable borderline peripancreatic lymphadenopathy.
At age 71, completed 10 cycles of FOLFIRINOX/modified FOLFIRI; transitioned to chemoradiation.

At age 71, patient received concurrent chemoradiation with capecitabine 825 mg/m2 twice daily on radiation days; 50.4 Gy in 28 fractions delivered to the pancreatic body mass and regional lymph nodes using intensity-modulated radiation therapy.
At age 71, during chemoradiation, patient experienced grade 2 nausea, grade 1 diarrhea, and grade 2 fatigue; managed with supportive care; completed all 28 fractions.

At age 72, restaging CT 6 weeks post-chemoradiation demonstrated further decrease in the pancreatic body mass to 2.4 cm with radiation-related inflammatory changes; persistent celiac artery contact but no definite encasement; tumor board deemed still unresectable.

At age 72, CA 19-9 post-chemoradiation was 94 U/mL; discussion of maintenance therapy with BRCA2-directed olaparib considered; patient enrolled in clinical trial evaluating olaparib maintenance in BRCA-mutated pancreatic cancer.
At age 72, patient began maintenance olaparib 300 mg twice daily as part of clinical trial protocol following completion of first-line platinum-based chemotherapy and chemoradiation.
At age 72, surveillance CT at 4 months on olaparib maintenance showed stable pancreatic body mass at 2.3 cm; no new lesions; CA 19-9 stable at 78 U/mL.
At age 72, patient developed grade 2 anemia on olaparib with hemoglobin of 9.2 g/dL; managed with dose reduction of olaparib to 250 mg twice daily.

At age 72, patient maintaining ECOG performance status 1; weight stable at 142 lbs; pain controlled with long-acting morphine and gabapentin for neuropathy; continues pancreatic enzyme supplementation.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
At age 71, CT abdomen/pelvis with pancreas protocol revealed a 4.8 cm hypodense mass centered in the pancreatic body with encasement of the celiac artery and greater than 180-degree contact with the SMA; upstream pancreatic duct dilation to 7 mm; no hepatic metastases; several borderline enlarged peripancreatic lymph nodes measuring up to 1.4 cm in short axis.

Please correlate clinically.

At age 71, EUS performed showing a 4.6 x 3.9 cm hypoechoic mass in the pancreatic body with celiac artery encasement confirmed; FNA performed with multiple passes using 22-gauge needle; rapid on-site evaluation confirmed adequate cellularity.

Please correlate clinically.

At age 71, CA 19-9 at diagnosis was 1,247 U/mL; CEA 9.8 ng/mL; clinical staging cT4N1M0, Stage III; ECOG performance status 1; multidisciplinary tumor board recommended neoadjuvant FOLFIRINOX with consideration for platinum sensitivity given BRCA2 mutation; genetic counseling referral placed.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

scenario_blurb: Locally advanced or borderline resectable pancreatic adenocarcinoma. Treated with neoadjuvant FOLFIRINOX, some achieving resectability and undergoing Whipple procedure, some with disease progression. Include CA 19-9 monitoring, CT imaging response assessment.
